The Frontend Dev Forced to Eat Mango (1): Meeting MongoDB and Installing It

Time for another learning log, and this time it's all about MongoDB. Since "Mongo" sounds a lot like "mango" (芒果) in Chinese, I went ahead and named this series "The Fullstack Dev Forced to Eat Mango."
So what is MongoDB? MongoDB is a document-oriented (
) database management system, and it's what we call a NoSQL database.

NoSQL is also known as a non-relational database. Take SQL (a relational database) as a comparison: SQL mainly stores data in table form (Tables), while NoSQL stores data as JSON. That's why you'll see JSON formatting all over the place in NoSQL.
Quick side note: in MongoDB it's not actually called JSON, it's called BSON.
If you're not sure where JSON comes from, you can check out an earlier post I wrote,
(article in Mandarin), so I won't repeat all of that here.
So what's BSON? BSON stands for Binary JSON.

The whole point is faster parsing and better performance, so it switches to a binary encoding instead. Because of that, Binary JSON supports quite a lot of data types. Per the official docs, here they are:
Compared to plain JSON, it can store way more data types. After all, JSON can only store String, Boolean, Number, and Array.

And since BSON uses binary, it's not human-readable, only a computer can read it. JSON, on the other hand, is readable by both humans and computers. So we can wrap this up with a conclusion: MongoDB is a NoSQL database system that stores data in BSON format.
Installing MongoDB is super easy. If you're on Mac, you just need Homebrew and you can run the following commands in order to get it installed:
language-cmd shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast

brew tap mongodb/brew
brew install mongodb-community@5.0
If you don't know what Homebrew is, you can check out
(article in Mandarin) where I introduced it.
to download the installer. The install process is basically just clicking Next over and over.
